articles.title: Orioles score 6 runs in the 1st inning to beat the Red Sox 8-2
articles.description:
Brayan Bello (2-6) continued to struggle as a starter, allowing seven hits and eight runs in five innings.

The post Orioles score 6 runs in the 1st inning to beat the Red Sox 8-2 appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Orioles scored 6 runs in the 1st inning against the Red Sox on June 4.
Brayan Bello allowed 8 runs in 5 innings, worsening his record to 2-6.
Red Sox's loss impacts their standings and fan morale in Boston.

articles.title: Ex-national security adviser John Bolton will plead guilty in classified information case: AP source
articles.description:
The deal would resolve a criminal case filed in October that charged Bolton with 18 counts of either retaining or disseminating classified information, including diary-like notes from his time in government that officials say he shared with his family members as he was preparing a memoir about his time in office.

The post Ex-national security adviser John Bolton will plead guilty in classified information case: AP source appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: John Bolton will plead guilty to 18 counts of mishandling classified information.
Bolton's case involves sharing sensitive notes with family while writing a memoir.
The plea deal could influence future handling of classified information in government.

articles.title: 2 of the best boat tours in America are in Maine, according to USA Today readers
articles.description:
Plan your next Maine adventure.

The post 2 of the best boat tours in America are in Maine, according to USA Today readers appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: USA Today readers ranked two Maine boat tours among the best in America.
Boston residents can explore these tours as part of summer travel plans.
Increased tourism to Maine may benefit local businesses and attract Boston visitors.

articles.title: As the World Cup comes to Foxborough, homeowners are renting out their lawns for parking
articles.description:
Some residents near Gillette Stadium are renting out their yards and driveways for parking spaces during the World Cup, hoping to profit from the influx of visitors.

The post As the World Cup comes to Foxborough, homeowners are renting out their lawns for parking appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Homeowners near Gillette Stadium are renting lawns for parking during the World Cup.
Residents are charging for parking spaces to accommodate thousands of visitors in June 2026.
This trend could provide significant income for local homeowners amid increased tourism.

articles.title: Hopkinton police seeking information after ‘suspicious video’ of person walking into roadway at night circulates online
articles.description:
"Driving home on this road off 85 in Hopkinton around 11:30 last night and this dude in a construction vest and hard hat holding a shovel is just walking onto the road in front of me. He also had some Halloween mask on," a Reddit post said.

The post Hopkinton police seeking information after ‘suspicious video’ of person walking into roadway at night circulates online appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Hopkinton police investigate a suspicious video of a person on the roadway at 11:30 PM.
Witness described the individual wearing a construction vest, hard hat, and Halloween mask.
Residents are urged to report any similar sightings for public safety.
